member.name: M:business_smart.QueryDocumentsClient.QueryUsers(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: GİB'e kayıtlı E-Fatura mükelleflerini VKN/TCKN ile sorgular.

member.name: T:business_smart.DataSet1
member.summary: Represents a strongly typed in-memory cache of data.

member.name: T:business_smart.DataSet1.aEmirAjandaDataTable
member.summary: Represents the strongly named DataTable class.

member.name: T:business_smart.DataSet1.aEmirAjandaRow
member.summary: Represents strongly named DataRow class.

member.name: T:business_smart.DataSet1.aEmirAjandaRowChangeEvent
member.summary: Row event argument class

member.name: T:business_smart.frm_Table_Edit
member.summary: OLD

member.name: T:business_smart.tbHaber
member.summary: Represents a strongly typed in-memory cache of data.

member.name: T:business_smart.tbHaber.tbHaberDataTable
member.summary: Represents the strongly named DataTable class.

member.name: T:business_smart.tbHaber.tbHaberRow
member.summary: Represents strongly named DataRow class.

member.name: T:business_smart.tbHaber.tbHaberRowChangeEvent
member.summary: Row event argument class

member.name: T:business_smart.tbHaberTableAdapters.tbHaberTableAdapter
member.summary: Represents the connection and commands used to retrieve and save data.

member.name: T:business_smart.tbHaberTableAdapters.TableAdapterManager
member.summary: TableAdapterManager is used to coordinate TableAdapters in the dataset to enable Hierarchical Update scenarios

member.name: M:business_smart.tbHaberTableAdapters.TableAdapterManager.UpdateUpdatedRows(business_smart.tbHaber,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Data.DataRow},System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Data.DataRow})
member.summary: Update rows in top-down order.

member.name: M:business_smart.tbHaberTableAdapters.TableAdapterManager.UpdateInsertedRows(business_smart.tbHaber,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Data.DataRow})
member.summary: Insert rows in top-down order.

member.name: M:business_smart.tbHaberTableAdapters.TableAdapterManager.UpdateDeletedRows(business_smart.tbHaber,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Data.DataRow})
member.summary: Delete rows in bottom-up order.

member.name: M:business_smart.tbHaberTableAdapters.TableAdapterManager.GetRealUpdatedRows(System.Data.DataRow[],System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Data.DataRow})
member.summary: Remove inserted rows that become updated rows after calling TableAdapter.Update(inserted rows) first

member.name: M:business_smart.tbHaberTableAdapters.TableAdapterManager.UpdateAll(business_smart.tbHaber)
member.summary: Update all changes to the dataset.

member.name: T:business_smart.tbHaberTableAdapters.TableAdapterManager.UpdateOrderOption
member.summary: Update Order Option

member.name: T:business_smart.tbHaberTableAdapters.TableAdapterManager.SelfReferenceComparer
member.summary: Used to sort self-referenced table's rows

member.name: T:business_smart.UserSettingsException
member.summary: Exception indicating that an error occured while saving or restoring user settings data.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsException.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new exception.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsException.#ctor(System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Creates a new exception with an inner exception.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsException.#ctor(System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationInfo,System.Runtime.Serialization.StreamingContext)
member.summary: Required because System.Exception implements ISerializable.

member.name: T:business_smart.UserStorageOption
member.summary: Possible locations to save user settings.

member.name: F:business_smart.UserStorageOption.IsolatedStorage
member.summary: Settings will be stored in isolated storage.

member.name: F:business_smart.UserStorageOption.Registry
member.summary: Settings will be stored in the registry.

member.name: F:business_smart.UserStorageOption.File
member.summary: Settings will be stored in text files in the application directory.

member.name: T:business_smart.UserSettingsBase
member.summary: Contains base functionality for user settings storage.
member.remarks: To store user settings, inherit from this class to create your own settings class. Within that class use standard XmlSerialization rules to declare your fields and properties.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a UserSettings object that stores its settings in isolated storage in a section based on the class name.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.#ctor(business_smart.UserStorageOption)
member.summary: Creates a UserSettings object that stores its settings in the specified storage location in a section based on the class name.
member.param: The storage location for the settings.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.#ctor(business_smart.UserStorageOption,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a UserSettings object that stores its settings in the specified storage location in a section named per the parameter value.
member.param: The storage location for the settings.
The section name in which the settings should be stored.

member.name: P:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.Section
member.summary: The section name in which the settings are stored.

member.name: P:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.StorageOption
member.summary: The location where the settings are stored.
member.returns: The location where settings are stored.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.Save
member.summary: Saves the current settings based on the currently logged in user, the storage location and section name.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.SaveToIsolatedStorage
member.summary: Stores the user settings to isolated storage.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.SaveToRegistry
member.summary: Stores the user settings to the Windows registry.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.SaveToFile
member.summary: Stores the user settings to an XML file.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.Restore
member.summary: Creates a new settings object with settings data based on the currently logged in user, the storage location and section name.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.Restore(System.Type,System.String,business_smart.UserStorageOption)
member.summary: Creates a new settings object of the specified type based on the currently logged in user, the specified section name and specified storage location.
member.param: The type of the settings object to return.
The section name containing the settings.
The storage location of the settings.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.RestoreFromIsolatedStorage(System.Type,System.String)
member.summary: Restore the user settings from isolated storage.
member.param: The type of the settings object.
The section in which the settings are stored.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.RestoreFromRegistry(System.Type,System.String)
member.summary: Restore the user settings from the Windows registry.
member.param: The type of the settings object.
The section in which the settings are stored.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.RestoreFromFile(System.Type,System.String)
member.summary: Restore the user settings from an XML file.
member.param: The type of the settings object.
The section in which the settings are stored.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.GetAppName
member.summary: Returns the name the the running application.
member.remarks: Since this relies on the AppDomain name having some reasonable meaning, this method may not work in some situations. Most notably, it will not work in ASP.NET where the AppDomain names are unpredicable and have little to no meaning. If you need to store and retrieve user setting values in ASP.NET you'll need to enhance the UserSettingsBase class to have some other way of identifying the name of your application.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.GetUser
member.summary: Returns the name of the current user.

remarks.para: If there is no current user the .NET Framework supplies an empty GenericIdentity object, with an empty user name. If we get an empty user name we return 'default' instead.

If the user identity is a Windows identity the user name will include the domain name. Since we're using the user name to build a path, the '\' in the name will cause problems so we strip out the domain and '\' symbol.

member.returns: The name of the current user.

member.name: M:business_smart.UserSettingsBase.GetFilePath(System.String)
member.summary: Returns a full file path to a user-settings file.
member.param: The section name for these settings.
member.returns: A complete file path (path and name) for the settings file.
